cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to work through this question. So, the question is asking why Ms. Leibfried asked the author to recite a passage in front of the class. The choices are A through D. First, I need to go back to the text to find the relevant part. The author mentions that the big project was a book report to be read aloud. However, Ms. Leibfried suggested the author do something "a little different." Instead of a report, the author chose a passage from "The Bell Jar" to recite. Now, let's consider each option. Option A is to test memory. The text doesn't mention anything about memory being a focus. The author was worried about sweating and delivering the recitation, but there's no indication that the purpose was to test memory. So maybe not A. Option B is to make her earn respect. The author was shy and an introvert, being the only nonwhite student. After the recitation, the class applauded, and Ms. Leibfried praised her. It seems like the act helped the author gain confidence
